categories.emulation: P-code emulation (run functions with controlled inputs)

endpoints.description: Attach one or more tags to a function. Tags are comma-separated and will be auto-created if they do not already exist.
Get auto-analysis status for open programs
Trace value propagation through a function (PCode graph, forward/backward)
Composite RE documentation analysis (decompile + classify + variables + completeness)
Comprehensive single-call function analysis. Accepts function name or address.

Ingest a single function's documentation into the cross-version archive (re_kb.functions on bsim Postgres). Idempotent; field-level merge resolution happens on the archive side. Use archive_ingest_program for bulk.

Bulk-ingest every function in a program into the cross-version documentation archive. Posts each to /v1/doc_archive/upsert. Returns per-binary counts (created / updated / conflicts_enqueued / errors).
Audit a global variable's documentation state. Returns name, type, length, plate comment, xref count, and list of issues. Use this before set_global so you know exactly what's missing.

Audit every global variable referenced from within a function in one call. Walks the function's instructions, collects unique data references, and returns the per-global audit (same shape as audit_global) plus a summary of how many are fully documented vs have issues. The killer per-function pre-flight tool — start every doc pass with this when the function has global xrefs.
Attach tags to many functions in one transaction. Body: [{"function":"0x140200ae6","tags":"syscall,lpe-surface"}, ...]. Tags auto-create.
Batch analyze completeness for multiple functions
Apply all documentation to a function in one call
Decompile multiple functions at once. Accepts comma-separated function names or addresses.
Detach tags from many functions in one transaction. Body shape matches /batch_add_function_tags.
Report of source file strings and their FUN_* functions
Check if address can be renamed
Clear all comments for a function
Close an open program by project path or name
Create a folder in the project
Create a program-wide function tag definition with an optional comment. Use add_function_tag to attach it to functions.

Create structure. Body fields must be a JSON array of objects with name and type, plus optional decimal offset. Example fields: [{"name":"dwId","type":"uint","offset":0},{"name":"pNext","type":"void *","offset":4}]. Type may be any resolvable Ghidra data type or existing struct name.
Translate a runtime dynamic address from the current trace back to a static Ghidra program address
Launch an executable through Ghidra's Trace RMI debugger launcher
List available debugger launch/attach options for the current program
List all breakpoints in the current trace
List modules (DLLs/EXEs) loaded in the debugged process
Read memory from the debugged process. Returns hex dump and DWORD interpretation.
Read CPU registers from the current debug trace snapshot. Shows general-purpose registers (EAX-EDI, EIP, ESP, EFLAGS for x86)
Remove a breakpoint at an address
Resume execution of the debugged process
Set a software execution breakpoint at an address in the trace
Get the call stack backtrace for the current thread
Translate a static Ghidra program address to a runtime dynamic address in the current trace
Get debugger status: active trace, thread, execution state, module count
Single-step into the next instruction (follows calls)
Step out of the current function (run to return)
Step over the next instruction (does not follow calls)
Delete a file from the project
Delete a program-wide function tag definition. This detaches the tag from every function that had it.
Replace a structure field with an embedded struct type by value (e.g. Rectangle inside LayoutNode). Uses modify_struct_field internally.

Emulate a single function with controlled register/memory inputs. Returns final register state after execution. Ideal for understanding hash functions, crypto routines, or any pure-computation code path.

Brute-force API hash resolution. Emulates a hash function with each candidate API name and returns the one that produces the target hash. Ideal for resolving ROR13, CRC32, djb2, FNV, and custom hash algorithms.
Find gaps of undefined bytes between functions in executable memory
Cross-binary fuzzy function matching. On programs with multiple address spaces (e.g., embedded targets), prefix addresses with the space name (mem:1000) to avoid ambiguous resolution.
List all physical address spaces in the program

Get highlighted address ranges in the CodeBrowser listing (GUI only). Returns {program, is_empty, ranges:[{start,end,length}], min_address, max_address, num_addresses} or an empty-selection payload when nothing is highlighted.
Get data type size in bytes
Return the number of functions in the loaded program

Dump raw P-code for a function (issue #192). Returns low (basic-iter) and high (HighFunction) P-code with basic blocks and varnodes. Each block's 'pcodes' and the top-level 'high_pcodes' are compact columnar tables {columns,rows} (varnode cells stay full objects, so no SSA info is lost). Granularity controls output: 'basic' = basic-block iter only (less memory), 'high' = HighFunction graph (default; includes both BB iter and op-iter). For P-code emulators / ML pipelines / alternative decompilers.
List all tags assigned to a specific function. Accepts either a function address or a function name.
List all variables in a function. Accepts function_name or address (address takes precedence). Use address to avoid name-lookup issues after a recent rename.

Dump the program's language description: address spaces, registers, default symbols, endianness, pointer size (issue #192). Use for P-code emulators / ML pipelines that need the SLEIGH-level facts. include_registers/include_default_symbols toggle expensive sections.
Get info about the currently open project
Get data type size and info
Health check endpoint for headless server
Import a binary file from disk into the current Ghidra project and open it. For raw firmware binaries, specify language (e.g. 'ARM:LE:32:Cortex') and optionally compiler_spec (e.g. 'default').

List the member functions of a C++ class. A function counts as a member if it lives in the class's namespace (e.g. after set_function_this_type re-parents it) OR its implicit 'this' parameter types as '<class> *'. Each result reports how it matched (namespace / this_type / both). Replaces the manual 'search __thiscall functions then read each signature' workflow.
List data sorted by xref count
List all program-wide function tag definitions with their use counts.
List all function names with pagination

Load a binary file into the headless server for analysis. For raw firmware (no recognizable header), pass `language` (e.g. 'ARM:LE:32:Cortex') and optionally `compiler_spec` (e.g. 'default'); the file is then imported as raw binary with the requested processor. When `language` is omitted, the loader auto-detects the format.
HTTP server health: pool stats, uptime, memory, active request count
Machine-readable API schema with endpoint metadata

Bulk merge: copy all RE documentation (function names, signatures, plate comments, instruction comments at EOL/PRE/POST, non-default labels & global symbols) from one program to another at matching addresses. Server-side iteration in a single transaction. Designed for the orphan-rescue workflow: source is typically '<name>_recovered', target is the original '<name>.dll'. Set dry_run=true to count without writing.
Set a structure field's type by name or offset (offset:N). Same as modify_struct_field with new_type only.
Move a file to another project folder
Move a folder to another location

Open an existing Ghidra project (.gpr file or directory). GUI mode adds optional `headless` (default true) to suppress auto-launching CodeBrowser, and optional `program` to auto-launch CodeBrowser for a specific file when headless=false. Headless server ignores the extra params.
Get detailed project info including running tools and open programs
Temporarily enable, disable, or query scoped automation prompt handling
Trigger full auto-analysis on a program

Replace a structure in one step: optionally remove an existing same-named type, then create with fields JSON (same shape as create_struct). Use when resize_struct cannot apply or you are rebuilding layout from get_struct_layout export. Set force=true to delete a non-stub type that is not referenced.
Detach one or more tags from a function. Does not delete the program-wide tag definition — use delete_function_tag for that.
Rename a variable in a function. Accepts functionName or function_address; address is more stable after recent renames.

Grow or shrink an existing structure by total byte size. Defined fields whose end offset fits within new_size are preserved; growth pads with undefined filler. Refuses shrink that would clip defined fields unless force=true. See docs/STRUCT_RESIZE_WORKFLOW.md.

Find duplicate data types by simple name; delete unused /Demangler size-1 stubs when a larger canonical type exists. Helps fix 'Can't resolve datatype' and create_struct already exists on placeholders.
Run auto-analysis on the current program
List all functions that have a specified tag attached. Returns name + entry address.

Search for instructions by mnemonic and/or operand substring. Complement to /search_byte_patterns (byte-level); this matches after Ghidra has parsed instructions, so you can search for 'mov' + '[ecx+0xD0]' without knowing the encoding. Case-insensitive substring match on both fields. 'matches' is a compact columnar table {columns:[address, function, mnemonic, operands, bytes], rows:[...]} (zip columns with each row to reconstruct per-match objects).
Search defined strings by a regex/substring pattern
Set user permissions on a repository
Terminate all checkouts in a folder recursively
Terminate all checkouts on a single file
List all users on the server
Register server credentials for programmatic authentication
List all checked-out files in a folder, including server-side checkouts
List repositories on the connected server
Create a new repository on the server
Get file info from a server repository
List files in a server repository folder
Add a file to version control
Get version history for a file
Set a decompiler (high-level) variable or parameter type by name. Same as set_local_variable_type. For __thiscall 'this', prefer set_function_this_type.

Set function prototype (return type, param types, calling convention). NOTE: the function name in the prototype string does NOT rename the function — call rename_function_by_address separately if needed.
Update the comment/description on an existing program-wide function tag.

Set the decompiler/database type of the implicit 'this' pointer (ECX on x86 __thiscall/__fastcall). Use after set_function_prototype when decompile still shows void* this or a wrong class. Accepts 'MyClass *' or 'MyClass'. On programs with multiple address spaces, prefix function_address with the space name (mem:1000).

Atomically apply name + type + plate-comment + array length to a global variable. Single-transaction; rejects on validation failure with no partial write. Replaces the 4-tool chain (apply_data_type → rename_data → batch_set_comments → create_label).
Set the base address of the program (rebases all addresses)

Set types and names for multiple variables atomically. Types are applied first, then renames, in a single transaction. Hungarian prefix validation is enforced: the new name's prefix must match the type. On programs with multiple address spaces, prefix addresses with the space name.
Navigate CodeBrowser listing and decompiler to a specific address
Open a file in CodeBrowser, launching a new one if needed
List all running Ghidra tool windows
